root_text.an8.5:1.1: “Aṭṭhime, bhikkhave, lokadhammā lokaṁ anuparivattanti, loko ca aṭṭha lokadhamme anuparivattati.
root_text.an8.5:1.3: Lābho ca, alābho ca, yaso ca, ayaso ca, nindā ca, pasaṁsā ca, sukhañca, dukkhañca.
root_text.an8.5:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭha lokadhammā lokaṁ anuparivattanti, loko ca ime aṭṭha lokadhamme anuparivattatīti.

translation_text.an8.5:1.1: “Mendicants, the eight worldly conditions revolve around the world, and the world revolves around the eight worldly conditions.
translation_text.an8.5:1.3: Gain and loss, fame and disgrace, blame and praise, pleasure and pain.
translation_text.an8.5:1.4: These eight worldly conditions revolve around the world, and the world revolves around these eight worldly conditions.
translation_text.an8.5:2.1: Gain and loss, fame and disgrace,
translation_text.an8.5:2.2: blame and praise, and pleasure and pain.
translation_text.an8.5:2.3: These qualities among mankind are impermanent,
translation_text.an8.5:3.1: An intelligent and mindful person knows these things,
translation_text.an8.5:3.3: Desirable things don’t disturb their mind,
translation_text.an8.5:3.4: nor are they repelled by the undesirable.
translation_text.an8.5:4.2: are cleared and disappeared, they are no more.
translation_text.an8.5:4.4: they who have gone beyond rebirth <j>understand rightly.”
